item.title: Curse Warrior second trailer, latest details and screenshots
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Nippon Ichi Software and developer guruguru have released a new trailer, information, and screenshots for dungeon crawler RPG Curse Warrior. As announced earlier today, a demo is also now available for all platforms in Japan.

■ The Reality of Character Loss

When allies are defeated by enemies, they are not knocked out—they are “lost” on the spot. Lost allies will never return, nor can they be revived or resurrected.

Even allies you have spent a long time training can be lost with a single bad decision.

These are not unavoidable “accidents”—they are the results of your decisions.

Every decision you make in a dungeon carries the risk of losing your allies. Dungeons are randomly generated, so no two are the same, and their difficulty constantly changes. Danger is always close at hand.

—Allies killed by attacks are “lost” and will never return.

■ Irrational “Curses”

Curses that infest the dungeon will mercilessly bare their fangs at the player.

Your actions may be restricted. Your abilities may be stripped away. Your allies may be weakened.

The full effects of a curse are only understood once you experience it firsthand.

To survive, you must accept the curses’ irrationality and make use of them.

However, some curses can have beneficial effects, such as restoring health, dealing additional damage, or allowing you to deflect attacks with a parry. Survival depends on your ability to skillfully control curses.

—Curses with harmful effects can slowly drive adventurers to their deaths.

■ Strategy: “Who to Sacrifice?”

Jobs, party composition, items… players have a wide range of choices.

Who should stand on the front line? Who should stay in the back line? And sometimes, who should be abandoned? In Curse Warrior, strategy requires a willingness to make sacrifices.

There is not always a correct choice. Each job has strengths and weaknesses on the front and back lines, making their placement an important strategic decision.

—Clerics and Paladins, with high HP and durability, can take monster attacks from the front line.

■ Items

Before entering a dungeon, you can purchase items at the shop. Early on, you will have limited funds, so choosing what and how much to bring is key to survival.

—Provisions can be brought without spending funds, so make sure to bring them.

■ Demo Now Available

—All you carry over is knowledge.

A demo that lets players experience the irrationality and tension of Curse Warrior is now available.

All allies you train, equipment you acquire, and levels you gain will be lost.

All you carry over to the full game is knowledge of “how one dies,” “what will be lost if I act,” and “how one survives.”

And that knowledge is the most valuable resource you can have..

■ Few Clues Exist in This Irrational World

Those who enter unprepared will surely lose it all. Only the few who understand the world will survive.

Curse Warrior features systems that serve as the “keys to survival.”

■ The Role of Jobs

One who blocks enemy attacks on the front line. One whose skills pair well with curses. One who specializes in support, increasing allies’ chances of survival.

Each job has clear strengths and weaknesses, and none are all-rounders. Who stands in front, who is protected, and who is cast aside? Understanding jobs directly affects survival.

There are eight adventurer jobs, each with their own preferred attack types, curse resistances, and optimal party positions. Adventurers also possess two skills. Keep these characteristics in mind when forming your party.

At the “Wolf Moon Tavern,” you can hire a variety of adventurers, whose abilities differ depending on their job.

Hiring an adventurer with strong abilities or useful skills can be key to clearing a dungeon. Over time, new adventurers will appear at the tavern and become available for hire.

—Each adventurer has different abilities and skills. New adventurers visit the tavern over time.

■ Purification Facility: Rainbow Eyes

Rainbow Eyes is a purification facility that purifies curses.

By purifying curses, you can remove unfavorable curses while keeping beneficial ones.

Upgrading the facility increases the number of adventurers and curses that can be purified at once.

—Curses that disadvantage adventurers can be removed through purification.

■ Exploration Items: “Lifelines”

While exploring caves, exploration items such as potions, antidotes, and hammers are essential.

Use them, or save them?

That single decision could determine who survives and who is lost.

Dungeons offer a variety of items.

Some of these items strengthen adventurers when equipped.

Finding and equipping these items is essential to making adventurers stronger.

—Equipment can also be obtained as quest rewards or after battles.

■ Curse Fusion

Curses have affinities, and depending on their combination, they can become significantly stronger. This is called “Curse Fusion.”

Combining two or three curses creates a more powerful curse, transforming it into a higher-level curse with an increased activation rate.

You can carry up to six curses. If a curse has two effects, up to 12 different effects can activate. The timing and probability of each effect can cause the cursed character’s stats to fluctuate in complex ways.

Curses can have irreversible consequences. Only those who understand, accept, and are prepared to master them can take this gamble.

—Two curses can combine to form a new, higher-level curse.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/curse-warrior-second-trailer-latest-details-and-screenshots

item.title: Kirby Air Riders Gets A New Update Today, Here Are The Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Do you like bug fixes?

Kirby Air Riders got a new update towards the end of February, adding support for three more amiibo and making multiple balance adjustments and fixes.

As March races ahead, the dev team has rolled out a new update, bumping the game up to Version 1.3.2. As somewhat expected, it irons out all sorts of bugs and also makes "several other miscellaneous gameplay fixes".

If you are updating your copy, just remember that this latest version of the game is not compatible with certain replays from existing builds of the game. Here's the full rundown of this latest update for Kirby Air Riders:

Kirby Air Riders Version 1.3.2 (Released 11th March 2026)

Bug fixes

  • Fixed an issue in Free Run and Series matches within paddocks in which other players would sometimes be unable to progress to the next race or Stadium event if a player left or disconnected in the middle of play.
  • Fixed an issue with Hop Star in which the maintained speed would be higher than expected when jumping and landing via Quick Spin.
    • Maintained speed when performing a normal landing or perfect landing from a normal glide has not been changed.
  • Fixed an issue in Air Ride where players could increase Boost Dash speed by performing a slight, slow backward movement immediately after starting a match.
  • Made several other miscellaneous gameplay fixes.

Note: Ver. 1.3.2 may not be compatible with replays from Ver. 1.3.1 and Ver. 1.3.0. Ver. 1.3.2 is not compatible with replays from Ver. 1.2.0 and earlier. Convert replays you want to keep to video files by selecting Record on the playback-settings screen within Cached Data and Data Replay before downloading the update. (Note: A microSD Express card is required to use this function.)

In case you missed it, two new amiibo for Kirby Air Riders were also revealed last week. This includes Sword Kirby & Dragoon and Noir Dedede & Hydra.

A July release for Dedede & Tank Star has also been confirmed, and the Meta Knight & Devil Star amiibo arrived this week. You can find out more about the amiibo line in our previous story here on Nintendo Life:

Are you still playing Kirby Air Riders on the Switch 2? Have you tried out this latest update yet? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia Sales Surpass 2.2 Million Units Globally In First Four Days
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2 exclusive is off to a strong start

The relaxing life sim Pokémon Pokopia for the Switch 2 has got off to a roaring start, with The Pokémon Company and Nintendo today announcing global sales of the title have already surpassed 2.2 million units.

Pokopia managed to achieve this in just the "first four days" since its launch on 5th March 2026. Additionally, it's been confirmed one million of these units were sold in Japan during this time frame.

Specifics haven't been shared about the sales in other regions just yet, but the game is currently in the top spot in the eShop charts in locations such as the US and UK. There have also been reports about this new title being undersupplied for retail, and its early success has reportedly caused a surge in Nintendo's share price.

This is a respectable start for Pokémon Pokopia when you consider it's a Switch 2 exclusive and it's technically a spin-off of the mainline series. In comparison, Pokémon Legends: Z-A was reported as shifting 5.8 million copies in its first week, based on the combined Switch and Switch 2 game sales (approx 3.89 million sales to date on Switch 2).

Compared to other Switch 2 exclusives with over a million sales, Kirby Air Riders has sold 1.76 million units, Donkey Kong Bananza has shifted 4.25 million units and Mario Kart World is right out in front with over 14 million sales. The Switch 2 has now sold more than 17 million units worldwide.

If you haven't already played Pokémon Pokopia it's well worth a look. In our review here on Nintendo Life, we called it the "freshest Pokémon experience in a long time, bursting at the seams with charm and content that rewards both curiosity and creativity".

Did you buy this game in its first four days of availability? Let us know in the comments.

[source nintendo.co.jp]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Xbox’s Project Helix, Sony’s PS6, and the PlayStation Handheld Still Targeting a Holiday 2027 Release – Rumour
item.content:encoded:
• 0-20%: Unlikely - Lacks credible sources
  • 21-40%: Questionable - Some concerns remain
  • 41-60%: Plausible - Reasonable evidence
  • 61-80%: Probable - Strong evidence
  • 81-100%: Highly Likely - Multiple reliable sources

Today's slew of GDC 2026 presentations included a surprisingly interesting keynote talk from Jason Ronald, vice president at Xbox, providing some insight into the next generation of Xbox hardware with Project Helix. Included in that was the confirmation that the alpha versions of Project Helix's development kits won't be going out to developers until 2027, which sparked plenty of concern about the final release dates for the next generation of hardware. If game developers won't go hands-on with Project Helix until next year, what's the earliest players will get their chance? 2028? 2030?

Well, according to known AMD leaker Kepler_L2, the plan for both Xbox's Project Helix and Sony's PlayStation 6 is not to make players wait that long. Instead, the plan is still to have both devices out by Holiday 2027, as Kepler_L2 has previously claimed.

What's more is that the rumoured PlayStation Handheld device is also still set to arrive around the same time, according to Kepler_L2. They didn't offer any more information beyond the fact that all three devices still have that release window, but at least we have some indication that the next generation of video game hardware isn't another two or more years away.

With all that said, even with a leaker like Kepler_L2, it's always worth taking these claims with a grain of salt, especially when it comes to release timings more than anything else. The plan for a release date can change at the drop of a hat, and though it's very believable that the plan at the time of this writing for both Sony and Microsoft is to have their consoles out within weeks or even days of each other once again for 2027's holiday season, we've not even halfway through 2026 right now.

There's still a lot of time and a lot of things that have to go right to ensure that those devices will meet their targeted release dates, and amidst the ongoing memory crisis and tariff concerns, it's difficult to hang your hat on any hardware release windows.

Hopefully, though, Kepler_L2 is right. It would be exciting to be at the start of a new generation once again. Just be sure to save your pennies where possible, if the worst of the reports about how much these devices will likely cost are realized.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/playstation-6-xbox-project-helix-playstation-handheld-still-targeting-2027-release-rumor/

item.title: Bunny Garden 2 launches April 16
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch

Dating simulation adventure game sequel dated.

Dating simulation adventure game sequel Bunny Garden 2 will launch for Switch and PC via Steam on April 16 for $24.99, developer qureate announced. New characters Kuon and Runa were also introduced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its official website:

About

Another unforgettable night is about to begin…

Bunny Garden‘s brand-new title finally arrives!

This latest installment in the romance adventure game series is designed for gentlemen of pure heart who frequent the gentlemen’s retreat “Bunny Garden” and connect with its uniquely charming cast.

Reunite with familiar faces while welcoming new additions, delivering an even more immersive experience than its predecessor.

The “gentleman’s games” have evolved further; how you enjoy them is entirely up to you.

Forget the hardships of daily life and indulge in sweet, heart-pounding nights.

Story

Haito Kanda (name changeable) lost his job due to a major mistake at work, and had fallen into despair over everything.

Just as he was heading to his usual cheap pub to drown his sorrows…

A woman suddenly spoke to him, noticing his dejected expression.

That was the fateful encounter…

Guided by her, he stepped into a sanctuary for gentlemen, where women as adorable as rabbits welcomed him…

“Bunny Garden.”

What changes will this encounter bring to their lives, and yours?

It’s all up to you…

A new Bunny Garden, set in a different world line from the previous title.

A brand-new story and a cast you have yet to meet await you.

System

  • With a Drink in Hand, Enjoy Sweet and Delightful Moments With the Cast
    • Bunny Garden is a social gathering place brimming with relaxation and excitement for gentlemen. Savor an extensive selection of drinks and snacks while enjoying conversation with the uniquely charming cast.
    • Ordering menu items tailored to a cast member’s preferences might just boost your likability?
    • Furthermore, as the drinks flow and they become tipsy, they might reveal sides of themselves you’ve never seen before.
  • Allow Your Favorite Girl’s Song to Intoxicate Your Heart
    • Each cast member has their own signature song. You might find yourself captivated by that lively girl’s energetic performance!
    • Plus, when they sing while tipsy, you might just catch a glimpse of a surprising side you’ve never seen before…
    • Laugh, feel your heart flutter, and get a little excited. Come see the cast’s stage performances with your own eyes.

Characters

  • Kana (voiced by Mio Hoshitani) – With her carefree smile, she treats everyone equally. She is like Bunny Garden’s own sun. Her brightness and kindness unwittingly melt customers’ hearts. Yet behind that smile lies a strong resolve to pursue a certain dream.
  • Rin (voiced by Eri Suzuki) – The life of the party at Bunny Garden. Free-spirited and friendly, she talks casually with first-time customers to close the distance. Sometimes she teases like a little devil, enjoying the reactions—leaving many people flustered. But behind that free-spiritedness lies a slightly unexpected side…
  • Miuka (voiced by Masumi Tazawa) – The sharp-tongued one at Bunny Garden, who speaks her mind without hesitation. Countless customers have been stung by her piercing words. Yet there’s no malice whatsoever. Rather, her frankness and unwavering stance possess a strange charm that somehow becomes addictive.
  • Erisa (voiced by Yukina Shuto) – A cast member on her trial period. She tends to act aloof, but is actually a very kind and considerate girl with a tsundere personality. She has a severe tendency to overthink things, where even the protagonist’s casual words or gestures can instantly send her imagination running wild in that direction. She may seem strong-willed, but she actually loves imagining romantic scenarios and sometimes gets lost in her own fantasy world.
  • Kuon (voiced by Ruriko Noguchi) – Her words and actions are utterly indecipherable at first meeting—mysterious and bewitching, leaving you unsure if she’s joking or serious. One moment she’ll suddenly close the distance, the next she’ll drift away, leaving behind cryptic words. It’s as if she’s testing you. That very elusiveness is her charm.
  • Runa (voiced by Hina Nastume) – Her distinctive tone and slightly cheeky attitude make her stand out. She carries an enigmatic aura, using phrasing that seems to test people while intently observing their expressions and reactions. At unexpected moments, she flashes a knowing smile, as if she alone holds some secret, suggesting something deeper lies beneath the surface—something yet to be revealed.

Staff

  • Character Design: Waon Inui
  • Scenario: Takayoshi Muto (Synthese)
  • Producer: Yujiro Usuda

View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia Surpassed 2.2 Million Global Sales in 4 Days
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia continues to be a smash success on the Nintendo Switch 2.

Pokémon Pokopia, the new Pokémon spin-off, is a cross between a farming sim and a city builder, and has already made history as the highest-rated Pokémon game on Metacritic. It’s also already one of the highest-rated games of 2026. Now, Nintendo has shared news about the game’s global sales, marking even greater success.

Pokémon Pokopia Sells Over 2 Million Copies

Nintendo revealed in a press release that Pokémon Pokopia sold over 2.2 million copies in just 4 days on the market. The game launched on March 5.

“The Pokémon Company (HQ: Tokyo, Minato-ku, Japan; President and CEO: Tsunekazu Ishihara) and Nintendo Co., Ltd. (HQ: Kyoto, Minami-ku, Japan; President and Representative Director: Shuntaro Furukawa, “Nintendo” hereafter) announced today that global sales of Pokémon Pokopia for the Nintendo Switch 2 system have surpassed 2.2 million units (including 1 million units sold in Japan) in the first four days since its worldwide launch on March 5, 2026*.”

Learn more about Pokopia at this link. For more news on the future of Pokémon, check out Pokémon Winds and Waves. Those games will be released on the Nintendo Switch 2 at some point in 2027.

Have you played Pokémon’s latest spinoff? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Google Play Games for PC is getting more premium titles and cross-buy with Android
item.content:encoded:
Buy once (or more) to play anywhere

While Google announced last year it was opening the door to all Android games on Windows, things haven’t exactly worked out like that. It should have been easy, though. None of these “Windows” games is actually built for Windows—Play Games uses virtualization to run a lightweight Android OS in a container for the games. Hypothetically, all Android games should work, but there are still some big gaps.

For example, Play Games for Windows has thus far not supported paid games outside of those on Play Pass, and even some Play Pass content has been absent. In the latter case, that may be because developers have opted out. Google now says developers can choose to have Play Pass content available on both platforms. Regardless, the selection of free-to-play microtransaction factories in Play Games for PC hasn’t exactly screamed “premium experience.”

We should start seeing more paid games for Windows pop up, but Google’s going about it in an odd way. While these are still Android games at their core, Google is treating Windows as a separate platform. Thus, it has announced, “Buy once, play anywhere.” The idea is that developers can offer premium games in Google Play that include both Android and Windows access.

On mobile devices, anything you buy is always available on all other Android phones and tablets, but it’s apparently not the same for Windows. Developers have to join this program to offer cross-buy functionality, and it does not work for games you’ve previously purchased on Android. In addition, premium upgrades purchased on Android don’t necessarily carry over. Google says that depend son developer support and is unrelated to the new cross-buy program.

Google is making strides as it builds its desktop gaming catalog, but it still has a long way to go before it can attract any new players. In the distant past, Google might have just mirrored all mobile games on PC and called it a day, but Play Games on PC isn’t shaping up to be a Wild West. Google today is more deliberative and interested in controlling how apps are distributed. This is just another example of that mentality.

Updated 3/11 at 9PM ET with additional comment from Google. 



https://arstechnica.com/gaming/2026/03/google-play-games-for-pc-is-getting-more-premium-titles-and-cross-buy-with-android/
